report_name: FortiBleed: Massive Campaign Harvests 110M Credentials from 430,000 FortiGate Firewalls

threat_actors.name: FortiBleed IAB
threat_actors.description: A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) responsible for the FortiBleed campaign, using an automated pipeline for credential stuffing and brute-force attacks to compromise FortiGate firewalls. The stolen access and credentials are then sold to other cybercriminals, primarily for ransomware attacks.
threat_actors.goals: To harvest credentials from FortiGate firewalls at a massive scale and sell the resulting access to other cybercriminals for profit.

attack_patterns.name: Password Spraying
attack_patterns.description: Used in an automated pipeline with large credential dictionaries to systematically attempt logins against internet-facing FortiGate management interfaces.

attack_patterns.name: Password Guessing
attack_patterns.description: Used in brute-force attacks against FortiGate devices with weak or default passwords as part of the FortiBleed campaign.

attack_patterns.name: Network Sniffing
attack_patterns.description: The custom FortigateSniffer tool was used to passively capture credentials for 24 different protocols passing through compromised firewalls by abusing a built-in diagnostic command.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: Used to execute the legitimate FortiOS diagnostic command 'diagnose sniffer packet any ...' to capture network traffic as part of the credential harvesting process.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The FortigateSniffer tool captured credentials for various protocols like NTLM and Kerberos by sniffing traffic, effectively dumping credentials from the network.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The entire FortiBleed operation is designed to harvest valid accounts, which are then sold by the initial access broker to other threat actors, such as ransomware operators, for subsequent attacks.

malware.name: FortigateSniffer
malware.description: A custom Go-based network sniffer deployed on compromised FortiGate firewalls. It abuses the 'diagnose sniffer packet' command to capture credentials for 24 different protocols, including NTLM, Kerberos, and LDAP.

tools.name: Masscan
tools.description: Used in the reconnaissance phase of the FortiBleed campaign to identify internet-exposed FortiGate firewall management interfaces at scale.

tools.name: Shodan_Recon
tools.description: A reconnaissance tool used alongside Masscan to find vulnerable FortiGate devices exposed to the internet.

tools.name: mpbrute2.bin
tools.description: A brute-forcing tool used in the initial access phase to systematically attempt logins against FortiGate devices using large credential dictionaries.

campaigns.name: FortiBleed
campaigns.description: A massive, automated credential harvesting campaign active since February 2026, compromising over 430,000 Fortinet FortiGate firewalls by using credential stuffing and brute-force attacks to steal over 110 million credentials.
campaigns.objective: Harvest credentials from FortiGate firewalls at scale for sale to other cybercriminals.

identities.name: Fortinet
identities.description: Vendor of the FortiGate firewalls targeted by the FortiBleed campaign.

identities.name: Various Sectors
identities.description: A broad range of sectors targeted by the FortiBleed campaign, including IT service providers (MSPs), healthcare, finance, and government.

infrastructure.name: FortiBleed Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled server used to receive credentials exfiltrated by the FortigateSniffer tool from compromised FortiGate firewalls.

indicators.name: FortiOS Packet Sniffing Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: FortiOS Packet Sniffing Command Execution
id: 2b8c1f9a-3d7e-4b0c-8a1d-9f6e5c4b3a21
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the FortiOS packet sniffing command 'diagnose sniffer packet', which is abused by the FortiBleed campaign. Legitimate use is typically for short-term troubleshooting.
references:
  - https://www.bankinfosecurity.com/fortibleed-tip-iceberg-edge-device-targeting-a-32050
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1040
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1059.004
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains: 'diagnose sniffer packet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the FortiOS packet sniffing command `diagnose sniffer packet`, which is abused by the FortiBleed campaign to capture credentials.

indicators.name: FortigateSniffer Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: FortigateSniffer Tool Execution
id: 8e4a5b1c-9f0d-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the FortigateSniffer tool, a custom Go-based credential sniffer used in the FortiBleed campaign.
references:
  - https://www.bankinfosecurity.com/fortibleed-tip-iceberg-edge-device-targeting-a-32050
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1040
  - attack.credential_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/FortigateSniffer'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the `FortigateSniffer` tool, a custom Go-based credential sniffer used in the FortiBleed campaign.

relationships.target_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
